title: handle_send: relay send succeeds but insert_message fails — message_id returned is a random UUID, not correlatable with any stored record
description: In daemon/src/api.rs handle_send(): if insert_message fails (DB error), the handler falls back to Uuid::new_v4().to_string() as message_id and returns HTTP 200 with that UUID. The relay send already succeeded. Callers receive a message_id that does not exist in the JMAP store — any subsequent GET /api/messages?ids=[that-uuid] returns empty. This is a silent contract violation: the API doc implies the returned message_id is the persistent store ID. The message was broadcast to the relay but cannot be retrieved or correlated. Trigger: transient SQLite error during insert_message (e.g. WAL checkpoint collision, disk full).
